TU-UI review of UI-domain evidence at qualified baseline flow-state@a26f7fb. The RTM's transparent acknowledgement of the stale module docstring at input_handler.py:4-9 (which reads 'Global / Modals' in the wrong order) and the queued follow-up CR is the correct way to handle that documentation drift — runtime behavior is the authoritative truth and matches the requirement. (3) REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (UI-adjacent ToolContext facade) — tool_context.py confirmed; Tool base class at ui/tools.py:73 and the documented self.app = ctx._app backward-compat extraction at line 87 are accurate. Reviewed for the Simulation domain (REQ-FS-SIM-001, REQ-FS-SIM-002) at qualified baseline a26f7fb. Technical claims verify against source: SoA particle arrays at simulation.py:51-69 (correct), kick-drift-kick Verlet in integrate_n_steps (correct), spatial hash O(N) via build_neighbor_list at physics_core.py:48-97 (correct), is_static=3 tethered atom write at compiler.py:236 and is_static=1 static atom write at compiler.py:266 (both correct), Scene.rebuild at core/scene.py:421 dispatching to compiler.rebuild (correct), and the sync_entity_arrays / sync_static_atoms_to_geometry fast path at simulation.py:358-508 (correct). The two-way CAD-Physics coupling via tether forces (apply_tether_forces, simulation.py:427-454) is accurately characterized as the mechanism enabling entity-particle bidirectional influence. Minor non-blocking precision notes (offered for an optional tightening pass, not request-updates): (1) REQ-FS-SIM-001 cites integrate_n_steps as 'lines 100-180' but the function actually runs through line 251; the cited range covers the declaration and first half-kick + drift but stops short of the LJ force loop (~196-233) and the second half-kick (~236-247) that complete the kick-drift-kick cycle; consider 'lines 100-251' or 'lines 100+' so a reader following the citation sees the full integrator. (2) REQ-FS-SIM-001 says arrays are 'preallocated to MAX_ATOMS'; the actual attribute is self.capacity = 5000 (no MAX_ATOMS constant); and atom_color is (capacity, 3) rather than strictly 1-D. The SoA architectural claim is correct; only the prose phrasing is loose. Neither point rises to a defect under the inspection-based verification model — the citations land readers in the right function/file and the architectural properties are correctly stated. Recommending.

TU-SCENE review (orchestration domain). One substantive concern.

REQ-FS-ARCH-001 (Air Gap) evidence overstates 'sole authorized mutation gateway.' The narrative in Section 4.1 states that 'scene.execute(command)' is 'the sole authorized mutation gateway for persistent state' and that 'Cascade effects of commands ... are downstream consequences of authorized commands, not separate state-mutation channels.' This omits a known living asymmetry: Scene.paint_particles() and Scene.erase_particles() (flow-state/core/scene.py lines 452-491) mutate persistent Simulation state directly, bypassing the Command system. They are paired with Simulation.snapshot() per the Physics-Undo-via-Time-Travel pattern documented in CLAUDE.md Section 3.3, and they are deliberately exposed on the ToolContext (paint_particles, erase_particles, snapshot_particles at flow-state/core/tool_context.py lines 383-421). Simulation is persistent state (it serializes into .scn files via Scene.save_scene at scene.py line 650), so this is genuinely a second mutation path for persistent state.

The current evidence text would mislead a reviewer who asks 'is there any path that mutates persistent state without a command?' — the truthful answer is 'yes: particle brush, with snapshot-based undo as the alternative reversibility mechanism.'

Recommendation: Add a sentence (or short paragraph) to the REQ-FS-ARCH-001 evidence acknowledging the particle brush as an authorized exception that uses snapshot-based undo (per CLAUDE.md Section 3.3 'Physics Undo (Time Travel)'), not the command system. This parallels the diligence already shown in the v0.3 revision summary for other known asymmetries (interaction_data on Sketch, input-layer order, static vs. tethered atoms).

All other orchestration-domain claims verified against the qualified-baseline source at flow-state@a26f7fb: Scene.execute at scene.py line 184, Command ABC abstracts at command_base.py, CommandQueue historize handling at commands.py lines 78-110, Scene ownership of sketch/simulation/process_objects/commands at scene.py lines 65-89, ToolContext facade with private _app at tool_context.py, orchestration loop ordering and the constraints-or-interaction_data solver gate at scene.py line 324, Scene.rebuild() at scene.py line 421, _topology_dirty flag at scene.py line 293. Qualified-baseline commit a26f7fb matches actual flow-state/main HEAD with the cited commit message.

Reviewing as TU-SKETCH (CAD/Geometry domain). The qualified-baseline commit a26f7fb is verified, and the architectural invariants (Air Gap, Command Pattern, Scene/Session split, ToolContext facade) are correctly identified. However, three evidence narratives in my domain contain verifiable factual errors that should be corrected before approval, since the RTM is the verification audit trail and inaccurate evidence undermines its purpose:

1) REQ-FS-ARCH-002 (Command Pattern) cites 'DeleteEntityCommand' as a concrete example. No such class exists in the codebase. The actual class is 'RemoveEntityCommand' (model/commands/geometry.py:77). Please replace 'DeleteEntityCommand' with 'RemoveEntityCommand'.

2) REQ-FS-CAD-003 (Interaction Servo) states the Select tool injects mouse drag state via 'ctx.set_interaction_data(...)' each frame. The facade method exists on ToolContext (tool_context.py:427), but tools.py does NOT call it -- it directly mutates 'self.sketch.interaction_data = {...}' (lines 788, 950, 978, 1061). The requirement is still satisfied (the solver does treat the data as an infinite-mass constraint), but the cited injection path is incorrect. Please update the evidence to describe the actual direct-assignment path, or note this as a known asymmetry similar to the interaction_data placement note already in REQ-FS-ARCH-003.

3) REQ-FS-CAD-002 (PBD Solver with Hybrid Backend): minor framing issue. The evidence says 'solve() (the legacy Python/OOP backend) and solve_numba() (the kernel backend)'. In fact, solve() is a dispatcher that takes use_numba as a parameter and delegates to either the inline legacy path or to solve_numba(). The substantive claim (both backends exist, switchable at runtime via F9) is correct. Recommend rephrasing to 'solve() dispatches to either the legacy OOP path (inline) or solve_numba() based on the use_numba flag.'

Minor (non-blocking, illustrative-only): REQ-FS-CAD-001 evidence lists 'equal-radius' and 'distance' constraints as examples; neither exists as a distinct class. The requirement uses 'e.g.' so this is illustrative, but please align the examples with actual classes (Length, Radius, EqualLength, FixedAngle, plus PARALLEL/PERPENDICULAR/HORIZONTAL/VERTICAL via the Angle dispatcher class).

TU-SIM review: RECOMMEND. Independently verified all simulation-domain evidence against source at the qualified baseline. REQ-FS-SIM-001: Confirmed flat-array SoA layout in engine/simulation.py lines 51-69 (capacity=5000 default at line 40, atom_color (capacity,3) at line 60); integrate_n_steps spans engine/physics_core.py:100-251 with the standard velocity-Verlet form; build_neighbor_list at line 49 and spatial_sort at line 254 implement the uniform-grid cell list. REQ-FS-SIM-002: Confirmed engine/compiler.py writes is_static=3 at line 236 (_add_tethered_atom) and is_static=1 at line 266 (_add_static_atom); Scene.rebuild() at core/scene.py:421 triggers compiler.rebuild(); sync_entity_arrays at simulation.py:358 and sync_static_atoms_to_geometry at 456 (cited range 358-508 is accurate). Cross-cutting: REQ-FS-ARCH-001 brush-exception evidence verified — Scene.paint_particles/erase_particles at scene.py:452-491 and ToolContext.paint_particles/erase_particles at tool_context.py:383-413 with snapshot_particles at 415-421. The CLAUDE.md §3.3 framing of brush as a documented authorized exception under the time-travel snapshot pattern is accurate and well-justified. Minor terminology nit (non-blocking): the RTM describes integrate_n_steps as 'velocity Verlet integration in the kick-drift-kick form'. The implementation is standard velocity Verlet (drift + half-kick / force-recompute / half-kick), which is mathematically equivalent to KDK leapfrog but conventionally distinct in nomenclature. Consider simplifying to 'velocity Verlet' in a future revision; not requesting updates. v0.1 corrections (RemoveEntityCommand, capacity vs MAX_ATOMS, integration line range, solve dispatcher framing) are all verified correct.

Evidence in the orchestration domain (core/scene.py, core/command_base.py, core/commands.py, core/tool_context.py, model/commands/geometry.py, app/app_controller.py) is accurate and traceable to verified line numbers and class/file assertions at the qualified-baseline commit a26f7fb. Key checks: Scene.execute() at scene.py:184 is correctly characterized as the principal mutation gateway with CommandQueue delegation and historize-driven stack insertion; the brush exception (paint_particles/erase_particles at scene.py:452-491, exposed on ToolContext at lines 383-413 with snapshot_particles at 415) is genuinely a documented authorized exception, with justification correctly grounded in CLAUDE.md section 3.3 (snapshot-based time-travel undo for irreversible physics); RemoveEntityCommand (model/commands/geometry.py:77, changes_topology=True with serialized-form storage on execute) is correctly named after the v0.2 correction; the Command ABC contract (execute/undo abstract, historize attribute) at command_base.py is accurately described; the Scene/Session split is faithfully reflected (Scene owns sketch/simulation/compiler/command_queue/process_objects; Session owns camera/selection/focused_element/mode/current_tool); the interaction_data asymmetry note under REQ-FS-ARCH-003 is appropriately scoped and the consistent direct-mutation pattern in tools is correctly characterized as transient state per CLAUDE.md section 4.2. QA review of SDLC-FLOW-RTM v1.1 (CR-112). Frontmatter: title='Flow State Requirements Traceability Matrix' (OK); revision_summary present and substantive — describes the three evidence rewrites and the qualified-baseline advance. Document structure follows the v1.0 template. Cross-checked the substantive claims against flow-state@ec450e2 source. Found multiple concrete line-number defects in the cited evidence that fail QMS-Policy §5.3 (Traceability — evidence must be traceable to a specific artifact). Concrete findings:

(1) REQ-FS-ARCH-004 evidence — property-accessor sites are SWAPPED. The RTM says 'the SelectTool property pair at lines 263-269 and the LineTool/CircleTool/RectTool/PointTool shared property pair at lines 727-733'. Verified at flow-state@ec450e2 ui/tools.py: GeometryTool (the shared parent of LineTool/RectTool/CircleTool/PointTool, defined at line 248) has its property pair at lines 263-269. SelectTool (defined at line 692) has its property pair at lines 727-733. The two cite-sites are reversed. Fix: swap the two parenthetical phrases so 263-269 is attributed to GeometryTool/LineTool-RectTool-CircleTool-PointTool and 727-733 to SelectTool.

(2) REQ-FS-ARCH-004 evidence — class-vs-module docstring misattribution. The RTM says 'The class docstring at lines 12-58 explicitly documents the contract: "Tools receive a ToolContext (self.ctx). They have no direct application reference (CR-112)."' Verified at ec450e2 ui/tools.py: lines 12-58 are the **module** docstring (specifically the 'Architecture' and 'Tool Contract' sections that span lines 12-58), not the class docstring. The Tool **class** docstring is at lines 84-89 and is short ('Base class for all editor tools. Tools interact with the application exclusively through the ToolContext facade (self.ctx). See the module docstring for the allowed access contract (per CR-112).'). Fix: change 'class docstring' to 'module docstring'. Optional: cite both — the module docstring at 12-58 enumerates the contract; the class docstring at 84-89 references it.

(3) REQ-FS-ARCH-004 evidence — Tool base class line range. The RTM says 'The Tool base class in flow-state/ui/tools.py (lines 90-118)'. Verified at ec450e2: the 'class Tool:' header is at line 83; the class body (with __init__/activate/deactivate/handle_event/update/draw_overlay/cancel) ends around line 120. Fix: change '90-118' to '83-120' (or '83-119' depending on how the trailing blank line is counted).

(4) REQ-FS-UI-002 evidence — handle_input dispatch range and rationale-comment lines are stale. The RTM says 'flow-state/ui/input_handler.py (lines 113-129) implements handle_input() with the four layers in order' and 'An explicit comment at lines 117-118 documents the deliberate Modal-before-Global ordering'. Verified at ec450e2: handle_input is defined at line 115; the four-layer dispatch block runs lines 115-136; the 'BEFORE global hotkeys! / Dialogs need to capture keyboard input before hotkeys trigger' rationale comment is at lines 124-125. The cited 113-129 / 117-118 ranges are carryover from RTM v1.0 that did not get re-anchored after CR-112 EI-8's docstring rewrite shifted lines. Fix: update to '115-136' for the dispatch block and '124-125' for the rationale comment.

(5) REQ-FS-UI-002 evidence — input_handler.py docstring line range. The RTM says 'The input_handler.py module docstring at lines 4-9 was corrected in CR-112 EI-8'. Verified at ec450e2: the corrected module docstring spans lines 1-16; the protocol-numbering portion (the actual content that documents the order) is at lines 4-11. The cited 'lines 4-9' is the OLD docstring's range from before the EI-8 rewrite. Fix: update to 'lines 1-16' (full docstring) or 'lines 4-11' (the protocol numbering specifically).

Non-findings (verified accurate):
- Frontmatter title and revision_summary fields present and meaningful.
- §5 Qualified Baseline commit hash 'ec450e2' is a real commit (verified via git show); commit message 'CR-112 EI-8: Update stale module docstrings' matches the actual commit message exactly.
- §1, §2, §3 (Traceability Summary table), §6 References — all OK; CR-112 added; RS v3.0 reference correct (RS at EFFECTIVE v3.0 verified via qms status).
- REQ-FS-ARCH-001 evidence: scene.py paint_particles=452 / erase_particles=477 (range 452-491 verified); tool_context.py paint=383 / erase=400 / snapshot=415 (range 383-413 / 415-421 verified).
- REQ-FS-CAD-003 evidence — interaction_data call sites all verified at ec450e2 ui/tools.py: ctx.set_interaction_data with point_idx at line 963 (in _start_edit_drag); ctx.set_interaction_data with handle_t at line 1041; ctx.update_interaction_target(curr_sim) at line 914; ctx.clear_interaction_data() at line 774 (cancel path) and line 935 (release path); ctx.has_interaction_data() at line 913. All six citations are exact.
- REQ-FS-CAD-003 — Solver._solve_interaction call site at solver.py line 42 (definition is at line 718; the cited '_solve_interaction at line 42' is the call site, which is defensible but could be clarified).
- REQ-FS-SIM-001 evidence — simulation.py particle arrays at lines 51-69 verified; capacity=5000 at line 40 verified; atom_color (capacity, 3) at line 60 verified.
- REQ-FS-SIM-002 evidence — compiler.py is_static=1 line 266 / is_static=3 line 236; scene.py rebuild() line 421 — all consistent with v1.0 (which was already verified) and the Compiler module unchanged in CR-112's scope (only docstring touched per EI-8).
- ToolContext API surface bullets in REQ-FS-ARCH-004 — all the listed methods exist at ec450e2 (add_process_object at line 427; set_interaction_data at 443; update_interaction_target at 471; clear_interaction_data at 481; has_interaction_data at 485; clear_constraint_ui at 503; create_coincident_command at 523; iter_entities at 551).

Note on Scene §3.24: the RTM also says 'Scene's own read of interaction_data at core/scene.py is a deliberate non-policed exception'. Verified at ec450e2 scene.py: line 324 reads 'if self.sketch.constraints or self.sketch.interaction_data:'. The claim is accurate; an explicit line cite (scene.py:324) would tighten the evidence but is not required.

The core technical claims of the v1.1 RTM are correct and the qualified baseline is real. The defects are line-number drift in evidence that fails the traceability test — a third-party reviewer reading the cited line ranges in the source would find different content than the RTM describes for items (1), (2), (3), (4), (5). Per QMS-Policy §5.3, evidence must be 'traceable to a specific artifact'; these citations point to the wrong artifacts.

Recommend: revise to fix the five findings above; re-route. Other reviewers (tu_ui, tu_sketch, tu_scene) have been assigned and may surface domain-specific concerns of their own.

ARCHITECTURAL OBSERVATION ON UNDERSCORE CONVENTION (not a finding; offered as forward signal):
The v1.1 evidence frames ctx._get_sketch() and ctx._get_scene() as 'internal-only by convention' while simultaneously documenting them as the sanctioned subclass-property delegation path used by GeometryTool and SelectTool. This is technically accurate at ec450e2 and satisfies REQ-FS-ARCH-004 as written ('no self.app or equivalent passthrough' — met). However, the convention is dual-signaling: a reader seeing self.ctx._get_sketch() in a property accessor reasonably asks 'if this is internal, why am I using it from a subclass?' The Air Gap is preserved by discipline (tools must not mutate the returned Sketch directly, but only pass it into Command constructors), not by structural enforcement — once a tool has a Sketch reference, it can in principle reach sketch.entities, sketch.constraints, sketch.interaction_data, etc. The strengthening of REQ-FS-CAD-003 (adding explicit set/update/clear/has facade methods for interaction_data) is precisely the pattern that addresses this residual hole, applied to the most active reach-through site. A future CR could complete the symmetry by either (a) renaming _get_sketch / _get_scene to first-class facade methods with explicit 'for command construction' / 'for solver coordination' names and removing the underscore (the WARNING-on-public-name pattern already exists in tool_context.py for get_entity_direct), or (b) eliminating the subclass property accessors entirely so command-construction sites call ctx._get_sketch() inline with a lint rule. Neither is required for v1.1 acceptance — the RS v3.0 requirement is satisfied — but the dual-signaling is worth a backlog entry for a future architectural CR. This is an observation, not a request for changes to the v1.1 RTM.
